```python
# Get constants from metadata
input_a = metadata["input_a"]  # "100101"
input_b = metadata["input_b"]  # "011010"
expected_sum_bits = metadata["expected_sum_bits"]  # "0111111"
expected_output_bitstring = metadata["expected_output_bitstring"]  # "01111111001010"

# Verify addend register restoration and correct sum
# Addend register bits (positions 7-12) should match INPUT_A
# Carry-in bit (position 13) should be '0' (carry-in bit was '0' initially)
# Sum bits (positions 0-6) should match EXPECTED_SUM_BITS
assert all(bitstring[7:13] == input_a for bitstring in counts.keys()), f"Addend register not restored correctly"
assert all(bitstring[13] == '0' for bitstring in counts.keys()), f"Carry-in bit not restored to '0'"
assert all(bitstring[:7] == expected_sum_bits for bitstring in counts.keys()), f"Sum bits incorrect"
assert all(bitstring == expected_output_bitstring for bitstring in counts.keys()), f"Full output bitstring mismatch"
```

```python
# Extract constants from metadata
expected_output = metadata["expected_output_bitstring"]
input_a = metadata["input_a"]
input_b = metadata["input_b"]
expected_sum = metadata["expected_sum_bits"]

# Validate counts dictionary
assert isinstance(counts, dict), "counts must be a dictionary"
assert all(isinstance(key, str) and len(key) == 14 for key in counts.keys()), "All keys must be 14-bit strings"
assert sum(counts.values()) == shots, f"Total counts {sum(counts.values())} must equal shots {shots}"

# The first 7 bits (0-6) should be the sum bits, last 6 bits (7-12) should be B restored, bit 13 is carry
for bitstring, count in counts.items():
    # Check sum bits match expected sum
    sum_bits = bitstring[0:7]
    assert sum_bits == expected_sum, f"Sum bits {sum_bits} don't match expected {expected_sum} for bitstring {bitstring}"
    
    # Check B register is restored (bits 7-12)
    b_restored = bitstring[7:13]
    assert b_restored == input_b, f"B register {b_restored} not restored to original {input_b}"
    
    # Check carry-in bit is restored to 0 (bit 6 was originally 0)
    assert bitstring[13] == '0', f"Carry-in bit not restored to 0: {bitstring}"

# Verify that the expected output bitstring appears in counts
assert expected_output in counts, f"Expected output {expected_output} not found in counts"
```

```python
assert 'counts' in locals(), "counts variable must be defined"
assert isinstance(counts, dict), "counts must be a dictionary"

# Expected sum bits from metadata (provided in run_program output)
expected_sum_bits = "0111111"
expected_input_a = "100101"
expected_input_b = "011010"
carry_in_bit = "1"  # From circuit.x(1) initialization

# Check that all measured bitstrings have the correct format
for bitstring, freq in counts.items():
    # Verify length matches QUBIT_COUNT
    assert len(bitstring) == 14, f"Bitstring length mismatch: {bitstring}"
    
    # Extract relevant registers from the bitstring (qubits 0-13)
    # qubits 7-12: sum bits (6 bits) + carry_out (bit 13)
    # qubits 1-6: should restore input_b
    # qubit 0: should restore carry_in
    
    # Sum result check (qubits 7-12 = sum bits, qubit 13 = final carry)
    measured_sum = bitstring[7:13]  # bits 7-12 inclusive
    carry_out = bitstring[13]
    
    # Expected sum is 6-bit sum plus carry_out = "011111" + "1" = "0111111"
    assert measured_sum == expected_sum_bits[:6], f"Sum bits mismatch: {measured_sum}"
    assert carry_out == expected_sum_bits[6], f"Carry out mismatch: {carry_out}"
    
    # Check input_b restoration (qubits 1-6)
    restored_b = bitstring[1:7]
    assert restored_b == expected_input_b, f"Input B not restored: {restored_b}"
    
    # Check carry_in restoration (qubit 0)
    restored_carry_in = bitstring[0]
    assert restored_carry_in == carry_in_bit, f"Carry in not restored: {restored_carry_in}"

# Verify total shots consistency
assert sum(counts.values()) == shots, f"Shot count mismatch: {sum(counts.values())} != {shots}"
```

```python
# Constants from source code
INPUT_A = "100101"
INPUT_B = "011010"
EXPECTED_OUTPUT_BITSTRING = "01111111001010"

# Assertions
assert len(EXPECTED_OUTPUT_BITSTRING) == 14, "Expected bitstring length mismatch"
assert sum(counts.values()) == shots, "Total counts mismatch shots"

# Verify correct output
assert EXPECTED_OUTPUT_BITSTRING in counts, f"Expected output {EXPECTED_OUTPUT_BITSTRING} not found in counts"
assert counts.get(EXPECTED_OUTPUT_BITSTRING, 0) > 0, "Correct output has zero counts"

# Check that all measured outputs have the correct pattern
# First 7 bits should be sum bits (0111111)
# Next 6 bits should restore INPUT_B (011010)
# Last bit should restore carry-in (0)
for bitstring in counts:
    # Skip if it's the expected output (already validated)
    if bitstring == EXPECTED_OUTPUT_BITSTRING:
        continue
    
    # For other outputs, ensure they don't have correct pattern
    # (since this is a noiseless simulation, only correct output should appear)
    sum_bits = bitstring[0:7]
    restored_b = bitstring[7:13]
    restored_carry_in = bitstring[13]
    
    # If any other output has correct pattern, that's an error
    if sum_bits == EXPECTED_OUTPUT_BITSTRING[0:7]:
        assert restored_b != INPUT_B or restored_carry_in != '0', \
            f"Alternative output {bitstring} has correct sum but wrong restoration"
```

```python
# Expected output bitstring from source code
expected_bitstring = "01111111001010"

# Sum bits (7 bits) are stored in qubits 0-6, addend B in qubits 7-12, carry-in in qubit 13
# Check that each measurement result corresponds to the expected output
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
assert total_counts == shots, f"Total counts {total_counts} does not match shots {shots}"

# The circuit should always produce the exact expected output
# This assertion verifies correctness and reversibility
assert counts == {expected_bitstring: shots}, f"Counts {counts} do not match expected {{'{expected_bitstring}': {shots}}}"

# Additional verification: check that sum bits match expected sum
sum_bits = expected_bitstring[0:7]  # bits 0-6
assert sum_bits == "0111111", f"Sum bits {sum_bits} don't match expected '0111111'"

# Check that addend B is restored (bits 7-12)
restored_b = expected_bitstring[7:13]  # bits 7-12
assert restored_b == "100101", f"Restored B {restored_b} doesn't match original B '100101'"

# Check that carry-in bit is restored (bit 13)
carry_bit = expected_bitstring[13]
assert carry_bit == "0", f"Carry-in bit {carry_bit} not restored to '0'"
```

```python
# Extract expected results from metadata
input_a = metadata['input_a']
input_b = metadata['input_b']
expected_sum = metadata['expected_sum_bits']
expected_output = metadata['expected_output_bitstring']

# Expected output should be 14 bits long
assert len(expected_output) == 14, f"Expected output should be 14 bits, got {len(expected_output)}"

# For each measurement in counts, verify correctness
for bitstring, count in counts.items():
    # Input bits (qubits 7-12) should be restored to original input_a
    assert bitstring[7:13] == input_a[::-1], f"Addend register not restored correctly: {bitstring[7:13]} != {input_a[::-1]}"
    
    # Sum bits (qubits 0-6) should match expected sum
    assert bitstring[0:7] == expected_sum[::-1], f"Sum incorrect: {bitstring[0:7]} != {expected_sum[::-1]}"
    
    # Carry-in bit (qubit 6 originally set to 1) should be restored to 0
    # Note: qubit 6 is at position 6 in the bitstring (0-indexed)
    assert bitstring[6] == '0', f"Carry-in bit not restored: {bitstring[6]} != '0'"
    
    # Full bitstring should match expected output
    assert bitstring == expected_output[::-1], f"Full output mismatch: {bitstring} != {expected_output[::-1]}"

# Verify total shots
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
assert total_counts == shots, f"Total counts {total_counts} != shots {shots}"
```

```python
# Check that the output encodes the correct sum while restoring inputs
assert "expected_sum_bits" in metadata
assert "expected_output_bitstring" in metadata
assert "input_a" in metadata
assert "input_b" in metadata

expected_sum = metadata["expected_sum_bits"]
expected_output = metadata["expected_output_bitstring"]
input_a = metadata["input_a"]
input_b = metadata["input_b"]

# Verify that the expected output appears in counts and dominates
assert expected_output in counts
assert counts[expected_output] > 0.9 * shots

# Check that the sum bits are correct
sum_start = 7  # bits 7-13 contain sum bits (0-based indexing)
sum_bits = expected_output[sum_start:sum_start + 7]
assert sum_bits == expected_sum

# Check that input register B is restored
b_start = 0  # bits 0-5 contain input B (reversed)
b_bits = expected_output[b_start:b_start + 6]
assert b_bits == input_b[::-1]  # B register should be restored in original order

# Check that carry-in bit is restored (bit 6)
carry_bit = expected_output[6]
assert carry_bit == '1'  # carry-in bit was initialized to 1

# Check total number of measured bits matches expectation
assert len(expected_output) == 14
```

```python
assert counts is not None and len(counts) > 0
expected_output = "01111111001010"
total = 0
for bitstring, count in counts.items():
    assert len(bitstring) == 14, f"Bitstring length mismatch: {bitstring}"
    if bitstring == expected_output:
        total += count
    else:
        assert bitstring == "10010101101000", f"Unexpected output: {bitstring}"
assert total == shots, f"Only {total}/{shots} shots produced correct outputs"
```

```python
assert counts is not None

total_counts = 0

for bitstring, count in counts.items():
    total_counts += count
    if bitstring == expected_output_bitstring:

assert total_counts == shots
```

```python
expected_output_bitstring = metadata.get("expected_output_bitstring", "01111111001010")
expected_sum_bits = metadata.get("expected_sum_bits", "0111111")
input_a = metadata.get("input_a", "100101")
input_b = metadata.get("input_b", "011010")
expected_carry_in = "0"

assert len(expected_output_bitstring) == QUBIT_COUNT

correct_key = expected_output_bitstring[::-1]
total_counts = sum(counts.values())
shots = metadata.get("shots", total_counts)

assert correct_key in counts, f"Expected key {correct_key} not found in counts"
correct_count = counts[correct_key]
assert correct_count == shots, f"Expected all {shots} shots to be {correct_key}, got {correct_count}"

measured_sum_bits = correct_key[6:13][::-1]
measured_input_b = correct_key[0:6][::-1]
measured_carry_in = correct_key[13]

assert measured_sum_bits == expected_sum_bits, f"Sum mismatch: got {measured_sum_bits}, expected {expected_sum_bits}"
assert measured_input_b == input_b, f"Input B not restored: got {measured_input_b}, expected {input_b}"
assert measured_carry_in == expected_carry_in, f"Carry-in mismatch: got {measured_carry_in}, expected {expected_carry_in}"
```
